goal: 优化 shlog status coverage freshness 避免重复扫描 raw sessions

summary.title: Optimize status coverage freshness scanning
summary.what: Changed shlog status so coverage freshness checks reuse source file metadata collected once per source/root during a single status invocation. Added adapter-level collectFiles, inventoryFromFiles, and snapshotFromFiles methods for Codex, Claude Code, and Pi sources, and added a regression test proving multiple coverage records on the same root trigger one source file scan.
summary.why: The previous implementation preserved correct freshness semantics but recomputed source snapshots by walking raw session files once per coverage record. Real indexes with many coverage rows made status stable seconds slower while find/read stayed fast. Reusing the same in-memory file metadata keeps the raw-session truth check while removing repeated disk walks.
summary.user_goal: 优化 shlog status coverage freshness 避免重复扫描 raw sessions

decisions.chose: Cache source file metadata by source/root inside collectStatus and derive sourceInventory, coverage snapshots, and requestedCoverage from that shared metadata.
decisions.rationale: This preserves the status contract and fresh/stale semantics while changing the cost model from repeated disk walks to one disk walk plus in-memory filtering.
rejected: Trust stored coverage fingerprints without checking raw session metadata

decisions.point: How to keep source adapters consistent
decisions.chose: Extend SessionSourceAdapter with collectFiles, inventoryFromFiles, and snapshotFromFiles and implement the seam for Codex, Claude Code, and Pi.
decisions.rationale: Status needs a source-agnostic reuse path, while each source still owns its own accepted-file policy and fingerprint calculation.
rejected: Move all source fingerprinting into a generic helper that would ignore source-specific acceptedFingerprint fields

decisions.point: How to verify the performance fix safely
decisions.chose: Add a deterministic unit test asserting multiple same-root coverage records call collectFiles once, plus run real-DB checkout status smoke as supporting evidence.
decisions.rationale: Wall-clock assertions would be flaky; call-count regression coverage locks the implementation shape that caused the slowdown.

rejected.alternative: Introduce a persistent status freshness cache
rejected.reason: It would add cache invalidation and migration concerns that are unnecessary for the observed slowdown.

rejected.alternative: Delete or prune older coverage rows as the performance fix
rejected.reason: That would change users' coverage evidence instead of fixing the repeated-scan implementation.

review_notes: Validation passed: npm run check (29 test files, 197 tests).
Focused validation passed: status and source adapter tests (28 tests).
Checkout smoke on the real local shlog DB reported 49 coverage rows, 887 source files, 3842 sessions, and 197060 messages in about 1.82s with npm run shlog -- status --json.
No npm release, version bump, global CLI update, or skill update was performed in this commit.

architectural_claims: Status freshness checking now has a per-invocation source/root file metadata cache instead of recomputing snapshots from fresh disk walks for every coverage record.
Each source adapter owns both source file collection and snapshot derivation from already-collected files, preserving source-specific filtering and fingerprint semantics.
behavioral_changes: shlog status output shape and fresh/stale semantics are unchanged.
Multiple coverage records for the same source/root reuse one raw source file scan within collectStatus.
security_implications: No new network, credential, raw-content indexing, or source-publication behavior was introduced.
migration_notes: No database migration is required; existing indexes benefit after the optimized CLI version is installed or released.
The global shlog CLI remains unchanged until a normal npm release/install update is performed.
